title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership
description: Multiple services declare the same queue with different durability and arguments, hardcode every operational x-argument, delete shared topology at startup, and turn precondition failures into reconnect loops. Learners cumulatively produce versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.
Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.
